resources.string: Automatically re-enables Video Boost every time Pixel Camera opens in video mode. It only reads and taps controls inside Pixel Camera, nothing else.
Video Boost will turn on by itself every time you open the Camera in video mode.
The service is ready, but automation is paused. Flip the switch to resume.
Enable the accessibility service to let the app flip the Video Boost toggle for you.
Service enabled ✓. Tap to review the steps
Two quick permissions and you\'re done. The warnings Android shows along the way are normal for this kind of app; nothing is wrong.
Tap the button below. In Accessibility, turn on \"Video Boost Always-On\" (it opens highlighted) and confirm.
Waiting for you to enable it… turn it on and come back here.

Android calls it a \"restricted setting\" for apps installed outside the Play Store. Open App info, tap the ⋮ menu (top right), choose \"Allow restricted settings\", confirm with your fingerprint, then enable the service.
Installing the APK and Play Protect blocked it? Tap \"More details\", then \"Install anyway\". It flags every sideloaded accessibility app.
Video Boost turns on by itself in the Camera. Come back any time to review these steps.
The service turned off for a banking app. Turn it back on above when you want Video Boost again.

Banks and other sensitive apps block phones that have an active accessibility service (anti-fraud). To avoid that, this app turns itself off when it detects one, so you\'re never blocked. A reminder notification lets you turn it back on with one tap when you\'re done.
Turn off automatically for sensitive apps
The service turns off automatically when a checked app opens. Banks and other sensitive apps are checked by default; uncheck any you don\'t want, or check more below.
Full-auto on: re-enables itself after a sensitive app.
Full-auto on (wireless ADB): re-enables itself after a sensitive app.
Smart reminders on: alerts you when the app closes.
Reminds you on the app opening. Tap to set up more.

By default the reminder shows when a sensitive app opens. With monitoring, the notification is silent while the app is open and only alerts you when you close it. With Shizuku, Video Boost also turns itself back on automatically — no tapping.
Shizuku is running — grant permission to Video Boost.
Shizuku not detected. See the steps below.
No Shizuku? Grant “Usage access” and you\'ll still get the alert when the sensitive app closes (you re-enable manually).
Get the free Shizuku app from the Play Store or its website.
Open Shizuku and start it via wireless debugging (Android 11+), a computer with ADB, or root. Shizuku\'s own guide walks you through it. You may need to restart it after a reboot.
Come back here, flip “Full-auto (Shizuku)” above and allow the permission Shizuku asks for. Done — Video Boost now handles sensitive apps by itself.
Wireless ADB paired and full-auto on.
Paired. Re-enable wireless debugging after a reboot, then reconnect.
Pair once to enable and re-enable Video Boost without Shizuku.

Instead of installing Shizuku, Video Boost can talk to your phone\'s own “Wireless debugging” directly. Pair once and it can turn its accessibility service on and re-enable it after a sensitive app closes — no second app.
In Developer options, turn on “Wireless debugging”. (Enable Developer options first by tapping the build number 7 times.)
In Wireless debugging, tap “Pair device with a pairing code”, type the 6-digit code here and pair. Host and port are found automatically.
After a reboot you must re-enable “Wireless debugging” once; then tap Reconnect. No new code is needed.
Couldn\'t find the device. Open the pairing screen first, or type IP and port manually.
Pairing failed. Check the code and that the pairing screen is still open.
Couldn\'t connect. Make sure “Wireless debugging” is on.
Uses the open-source libadb-android (includes spake2-android, LGPL-3.0). No data leaves your device.
Reminds you to turn Video Boost back on after a sensitive app.
It turned off because a sensitive app opened. Tap to turn it back on when you\'re done.
It turned off for %1$s. Tap to turn it back on when you\'re done.
Silent notice shown while a sensitive app is open.
Paused while a sensitive app is open. It comes back when you leave it.
Paused while %1$s is open. It comes back when you leave it.
The sensitive app is closed. Tap to turn Video Boost back on.
%1$s is closed. Tap to turn Video Boost back on.
It re-enabled itself now that the sensitive app is closed.
Video Boost processes video in the cloud through Google Photos. Each video is stored twice and uses data. Always-on means more of both.
Works on Pixel Pro models with Video Boost (Pixel 8 Pro and newer Pro models).
